author: Austin Crawford
title: Jamaica Fundraiser Dance Party with Duzie

author: Austin Crawford
title: Night Sky Viewing
summary: Lay back in the Hodge Preserve field while local naturalist narrates the season’s constellations, planets, and maybe a shooting star. Bring a blanket/towel, beach chair, flashlight, and insect repellant for your viewing comfort. If the night sky is obscured by clouds, fog, or rain the program will be postponed to the following night.
content:
Lay back in the Hodge Preserve field while local naturalist narrates the season’s constellations, planets, and maybe a shooting star. Bring a blanket/towel, beach chair, flashlight, and insect repellant for your viewing comfort. If the night sky is obscured by clouds, fog, or rain the program will be postponed to the following night.

Participation is limited, contact Kim to sign up, email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Back To The Future - Movie On The Beach
summary: Summer movie series continues with Back To The Future  (1985,  PG) at 8:30 pm on Town Beach.
content:
Summer movie series continues with Back To The Future  (1985,  PG) at 8:30 pm on Town Beach.  

Arrive early to get a spot with your beach chairs & blankets.  

We do not charge admission but encourage donations to keep this weekly event going all summer long.

author: Robert Lucier
title: July 4th Fireworks
summary: The annual fireworks display will now be at dusk on Saturday, July 1st.  The more the merrier for this patriotic event, so bring the whole gang and all the accouterments — chairs, blankets, picnics, and glow sticks for the kids.
content:
The annual fireworks display will now be at dusk on Saturday, July 1st.  The more the merrier for this patriotic event, so bring the whole gang and all the accouterments — chairs, blankets, picnics, and glow sticks for the kids.

Live music starts around 7:30 pm and the fireworks hit the sky as soon as it's suitably dark, probably around 9 pm.

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Fireworks
summary: The show starts at 8:45pm on a barge 2000 feet off Fred
content:
The show starts at 8:45pm on a barge 2000 feet off Fred
Benson Town Beach. Seen from any location, set up on the beach or in your backyard with friends and family.

We are very excited to announce that Ocean State Pyrotechnics will be putting on the fireworks display this year! They will for the first time, be shooting off the display from a barge off-shore! This will be very exciting!

If viewing from the water, please be sure to keep clear 1000 feet away from the barge for safety reasons.

Ring in the New Year with us!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Globe at Night - Nature Walk and Community Science
summary: Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats.
content:
Block Island hosts many cool community science projects. Join the fun, different subject and location each week, and help collect important data about Block Island’s special critters and habitats. 

 

Globeat Night, meet at Hodge Preserve.  (July18 back up for poor sky conditions)

 

Advance sign-up recommended but not required, Email kim.gaffett@tnc.org
